info.description:
A-Pay

Authorization in A-Pay can occur using two parameters `apikey` and `project_id`.

return_url.description:
Return URL with Query Parameters

Example parameters:  
- `status`: the result of the payment (`Created`, `Fail`, `Cancel`).
  - **`Created`** - parameter is passed in the link when the user successfully created a deposit but, for some reason, didn't complete the payment.
  - **`Fail`** - parameter is sent when the user encountered an error while creating the payment.
  - **`Cancel`** - parameter is sent when the user initiated payment on the payment provider's site but, for some reason, did not complete it.

The difference between `created` and `cancel` is that with `created`, the user hasn’t begun the payment process—they saw the payment details but chose not to proceed. In the case of `cancel`, the user started the payment on the payment provider’s site but interrupted it and returned to the merchant’s site.

Example of the returned URL: 
`https://example.com?status=Created`

These parameters provide insight into the stage at which the user interrupted the payment process.

paymentpage_url.description:
UPI payment app deep link

Our system, using the method Upi_fast, sends a link of the form `upi://`,
which in theory should lead to the opening of the desired application on mobile OS.
But, on mobile OSs, there are cases when the universal link doesn't work and opens the wrong application.
To fix it you can use the solution on your side:
[https://stackoverflow.com/questions/65273125/invoking-upi-apps-from-my-react-native-app-works-in-ios-but-not-in-android](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/65273125/invoking-upi-apps-from-my-react-native-app-works-in-ios-but-not-in-android),
i.e. for the specified mobile applications write the exact link → replacing `upi://` from the link with the following data for mobile solutions:


- `Paytm` → paytmmp://;
- `Phonepe` → phonepe://;
- `GPay` → tez://upi/.


And let the user select the desired solution first and then get the link for the specific app.
Use the payment app links in the mobile version. Use the same links converted to QR in the desktop version.

post.description:
Signature generation (signature)

When sending webhooks A-Pay also sends signature: specifically generated hash string that is created using a private key.

The signature itself is calculated as follows:

```php
$signature = sha1($access_key . $private_key . md5($transactions->toJson(JSON_UNESCAPED_SLASHES | JSON_UNESCAPED_UNICODE)));
```

a string of three parameters is passed to the sha1 function:
| Parameter    | Description                                                            |
|--------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| $access_key  | The public key is specified in the settings of the A-Pay technical support personal account |
| $private_key | The private key is specified in the settings of the A-Pay technical support personal account |
| md5(...)     | Hash from the MD5 function of the entire array of transactions in JSON format             |

As a result of executing this code, a string is obtained that cannot be faked without having a private key that is not passed in webhooks.

The client can compare the generated signature with the received signature from the webhook and thereby make sure that the data that came was actually sent, and not faked by scammer.

Funds should only be credited to your users based on the amount specified in the webhook.

The webhook will include the status of the transactions:
  - If the status is “Success”, the client should proceed with depositing the funds into the user account.
  - If the status is “Failed” or “Rejected,” no funds should be deposited, or the deposit should be canceled.

access_key.description: The access key is specified in the settings of the A-Pay technical support personal account

signature.description: The signature used to verify the validity of the postback. Concept of generation in the description of the deposit webhook

transactions.description: Information about the deposits made (one or more) is passed.

order_id.description: Deposit ID in the A-Pay system

status.description: Deposit status, if the deposit was successful, the status will be `Success`

custom_transaction_id.description: Transaction ID in the client's system

custom_user_id.description: User ID in the client's system

created_at.description: Deposit creation time, in timestamp UTC format

activated_at.description: Deposit activation date, in timestamp UTC format

default.description: If `{"status":"OK"}` was passed we consider webhook successfully delivered.

status.description: If `OK` was passed we consider webhook successfully delivered.

When sending webhooks A-Pay also sends signature: specifically generated hash string that is created using a private key.

The signature itself is calculated as follows:

```php
$signature = sha1($access_key . $private_key . md5($transactions->toJson(JSON_UNESCAPED_SLASHES | JSON_UNESCAPED_UNICODE)));
```
